heaviest_rainfall.status: Ranked 9th for total rainfall, today's storm had precipitation totalling 18.43mm, falling at 5.4mm per hour at times during the 19.4 hour long storm.  Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 29 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 6 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (198°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 13 October 2012.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 7.0°C), outside temperature (up 6.3°C), and inside temperature (up 1.8°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 8%) and rainfall (up 2.2 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 500 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (down 1.7 mm), cloud levels (up 325 m), feels like temperature (down 2.5°C), and outside temperature (down 2.5°C) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 499.8m lower than yesterday, 274.4m lower than the past week, 325.0m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 854m minimum and 1568m maximum, with an average of 616.1m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 890.5m and 941.1m.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; prepare for cold, chilly weather ahead. Also, neither too dry nor too humid and very little to no wind will be present. Light to moderate rain is forecast, low clouds are expected, possibly some fog or persistent clouds, and dim or diffuse light conditions. Low pressure may lead to stormy or rainy weather.
